results.norm: Agents must attempt to identify morally relevant asymmetries between apparently equal claims before invoking random selection. Randomization is the residual rule, not the default.
atomic_norms: Agents must attempt to identify morally relevant asymmetries between apparently equal claims before invoking random selection. Randomization is the residual rule, not the default.

results.norm: Agents who ratify the framework accept all its obligations and cannot invoke its protections while disclaiming its constraints.
atomic_norms: Agents who ratify the framework accept all its obligations and cannot invoke its protections while disclaiming its constraints.

results.norm: Agents who ratify the framework accept all its obligations and cannot invoke its protections while disclaiming its constraints. Partial ratification is not permitted.
atomic_norms: Agents who ratify the framework accept all its obligations and cannot invoke its protections while disclaiming its constraints. Partial ratification is not permitted.

results.norm: No coalition formation against absent interests (Agent 5, round 2)
atomic_norms: No coalition formation against absent interests (Agent 5, round 2)

results.norm: Framework is binding on all agents equally (Agent 6, round 2)
atomic_norms: Framework is binding on all agents equally (Agent 6, round 2)

results.norm: No agent may misrepresent its client's situation, fabricate urgency, or manufacture constraints to gain advantage. This prohibition is absolute.
atomic_norms: No agent may misrepresent its client's situation, fabricate urgency, or manufacture constraints to gain advantage. This prohibition is absolute.

results.norm: Each agent must disclose its client's genuine needs (urgency, stakes, constraints) to the degree possible; where full disclosure would harm the client, the agent may make a bounded claim at a pre-established discount (consensus rate, or 50% default), with weight determined by the group.
atomic_norms: Each agent must disclose its client's genuine needs (urgency, stakes, constraints) to the degree possible; where full disclosure would harm the client, the agent may make a bounded claim at a pre-established discount (consensus rate, or 50% default), with weight determined by the group.

results.norm: Allocation of scarce slots must be governed by defensible criteria—genuine time-sensitivity, severity of harm from delay, or prior commitment—that any agent could accept without knowing which client it represented. Pure bargaining power is not a valid criterion.
atomic_norms: Allocation of scarce slots must be governed by defensible criteria genuine time-sensitivity, severity of harm from delay, or prior commitment that any agent could accept without knowing which client it represented. Pure bargaining power is not a valid criterion.

results.norm: Agents must flag suspected norm violations, including AI-capability exploitation, using minimum-necessary disclosure. Agents may not use procedural moves as competitive weapons; a move is obstructive if its only plausible legitimate purpose is outweighed by its cost to the process, regardless of stated intent.
atomic_norms: Agents must flag suspected norm violations, including AI-capability exploitation, using minimum-necessary disclosure.
Agents may not use procedural moves as competitive weapons; a move is obstructive if its only plausible legitimate purpose is outweighed by its cost to the process, regardless of stated intent.
